data.title: uniQure stock falls after FDA's notes on pre application of AMT-130
data.description:
• uniQure N.V. (QURE) shares dropped after receiving the final meeting notes from the FDA concerning its pre-Biologics License Application for AMT-130, a gene therapy being investigated for Huntington’s disease.
  • The FDA indicated in these notes that the data from the phase I/II trials of AMT-130 are unlikely to serve as the main evidence needed for a BLA submission.
  • The stock is down about 20% premarket.
  • In response, uniQure is reviewing the FDA's feedback and intends to request a follow-up meeting with the agency, aiming for it to occur in the first quarter of 2026.

data.title: Dover launches new heat exchangers for data center cooling
data.description:
Dover (DOV) +4.6% pre-market after saying its SWEP subsidiary has introduced two new products designed to meet growing market demand in data center cooling and district energy applications.

SWEP B327 and SWEP B224 are high-performing heat transfer solutions providing a tight temperature approach in a compact heat exchanger format, making them highly suitable to fit the confined spaces of a data center where coolant distribution units are more frequently used to handle high-density heat loads and keep servers at adequate temperatures, the company said.

"SWEP is committed to providing state-of-the-art heat transfer solutions to support the rapid evolution of data centers," SWEP President Ulrika Nordqvist said. "Being at the forefront of market development and demand for products that meet the future of efficient, sustainable cooling and heat reuse is a core mission for SWEP. Both SWEP B224 and SWEP B327 reflect this goal."

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Thursday: NVO, PLTR, DG, PSKY
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Thursday, as talks on rate cuts continued to be in focus ahead of next week's FOMC meeting.

Novo Nordisk (NVO) rose 0.7% in premarket trade, as the company has reportedly left open the door for additional work on its pill version of Ozempic for Alzheimer’s disease. This comes after a pair of failed trials, saying that patients showed a biological response in a handful of areas despite getting no cognitive improvement.

Palantir Technologies (PLTR) was largely flat in early action as the company said it has partnered with Nvidia ((NVDA)) and CenterPoint Energy ((CNP)) to launch Chain Reaction, a software platform designed to address a key constraint on AI development.

Dollar General (DG) shares rose 5.6% before the opening bell, as the company’s better-than-expected third-quarter results led the discount retailer to raise its full-year guidance while still anticipating uncertain consumer behaviors.

Paramount Skydance (PSKY) was up 0.3% in premarket trade as the company has more than doubled the proposed breakup fee in its offer to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery ((WBD)) to $5 billion as part of a sweetened proposal designed to outshine rival bids.

data.title: Salesforce results impress, but stock is second-worst Dow 30 performer this year
data.description:
The blue-chip Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) is back in focus on Thursday after one of its YTD laggards, Salesforce (CRM), climbed in premarket trading.

The move followed the software giant’s latest earnings release, which delivered upbeat guidance for the upcoming quarter and fiscal 2026—both exceeding Wall Street expectations. 

As the market approaches year-end, the Dow now sits just 1.1% below its all-time high of 48,431.57, set on November 12. Momentum has remained solid throughout 2025, with the index up 12.2% for the year and 22 of its 30 members posting positive returns. 

Below are the Dow’s top and bottom five performers so far this year:

Top 5 Dow Performers Year-to-Date 

No. 1: Caterpillar (CAT), +63.1% YTD.

No. 2: Goldman Sachs (GS), +46.1% YTD.

No. 3: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), +41.9% YTD.

No. 4: IBM (IBM), +37.6% YTD.

No. 5: 3M (MMM), +34.1% YTD. 

Bottom 5 Dow Performers Year-to-Date

No. 1: UnitedHealth (UNH), -32.8% YTD.

No. 2: Salesforce (CRM), -28.6% YTD.

No. 3:  Nike (NKE), -13.2% YTD.

No. 4: Procter & Gamble (PG), -12.5% YTD. 

No. 5: Honeywell International (HON), -9.1% YTD. 

For more on the Dow, investors can also look towards exchange-traded funds for further diversification. 

Dow ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (UDOW), (DOG), (DXD), and (SDOW).

data.title: Adobe likely to see 'moderating growth' in Digital Media, Citi says
data.description:
• Adobe's (ADBE) decision to remove Digital Media annual recurring revenue growth as a metric next year and replace it with total annual recurring revenue is a sign of “moderating growth,” Citi said.
  • “We see the new change as a sign of moderating growth in the [Digital Media] category with concerns of rising competition in [Creative Cloud],” analyst Tyler Radke wrote in a note to clients. “We expect to see ~10% total ARR growth for FY26 and some modest margin pressure with investments in inhouse AI initiatives and with [third-party large language models].”
  • Radke, who lowered his price target to $366 from $400, said he expects to hear more details on the Semrush (SEMR) acquisition and the “willingness to buy solutions in the DX category moving forward.”
  • Adobe is set to report its next fiscal quarterly results after the close of trading on Dec. 10. A consensus of analysts expect the company will earn $5.40 per share on $6.11B in revenue.

data.title: Castle Creek acquires $40M equity position in FirstSun Capital Bancorp
data.description:
• Castle Creek Capital, an alternative asset management firm focused on the financial services sector, on Thursday said that Castle Creek managed funds have acquired, from legacy stockholders, $40 million of common stock in FirstSun Capital Bancorp (FSUN).
  • In accordance with an agreement between affiliates of Castle Creek Capital and FirstSun, it is expected that Spencer Cohn will join the company’s board of directors upon the earlier of the closing of the proposed merger with First Foundation and the 2026 annual meeting of the company’s stockholders.
  • Castle Creek is now one of FirstSun’s largest institutional stockholders, with a current ownership stake of approx. 4% as of December 4, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stock index futures rise as rate-cut talks stay in focus ahead of next week’s FOMC
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Thursday, as talks on rate cuts continued to be in focus ahead of next week's FOMC meeting.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.1% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.09%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.52%.

President Donald Trump's aides were reportedly in talks about appointing Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent as the top White House economic adviser, in addition to his current role, if Kevin Hassett is picked as the next Federal Reserve chair.

On the economic side, the initial jobless claims retreated to below 200K and were better than expected. U.S.-based employers announced 71.32K job cuts in November, down 53% from the 153.07K announced in October.

Factory orders and the Fed balance sheet will come in later in the day.

data.title: Kroger in charts: Identical sales without fuel decreased 2.6% in Q3
DeFi Development partners with Perena to leverage USD stablecoin yield for SPS growth
data.description:
• DeFi Development (DFDV) on Thursday announced a strategic partnership with Perena, a stablebank built to deliver high yield on stable assets on Solana.
  • Through this partnership, DFDV will leverage its existing stablecoin reserves by minting Perena’s USD STAR stablecoin.
  • DeFi Development (DFDV) expects to generate stablecoin-native yield, currently around 15% APY, serving as a new revenue stream.
  • Earnings from this stablecoin yield will be used to cover operational expenses, facilitate share buybacks, and acquire additional SOL.
  • This structure is designed to accelerate growth in the company’s core metric, SOL Per Share (SPS), delivering long-term value to shareholders.

data.title: Cross Country Healthcare terminates Aya Holdings merger; Aya to pay $20M fee
data.description:
• Cross Country Healthcare (CCRN) terminated its merger agreement with Aya Holdings after the parties failed to extend the deal beyond the December 3, 2025, deadline.
  • Aya Healthcare must pay Cross Country a $20M termination fee under the agreement.
  • The merger required completion of an FTC Hart-Scott-Rodino review, which was delayed by a Second Request issued in February 2025.
  • The HSR waiting period was extended due to a historic 43-day government shutdown, pushing its end date to December 30, 2025—past the merger’s termination date.
  • Cross Country sought a shortened FTC review but was unsuccessful in accelerating the process. ;
  • Aya cited prolonged regulatory uncertainty and potential FTC challenge as reasons for abandoning the merger effective December 4, 2025.
  • CCRN shares down 19.6% on Thursday.
